name.text: Atomic Structure Explorer - Build a Neutral Atom

questiontext.text:
Atomic Structure Explorer

In this activity you will investigate how protons, neutrons and electrons determine the properties of an atom.

Before you start

  • The number of protons determines the element.
  • The mass number equals protons + neutrons.
  • The number of electrons determines the charge.
  • A neutral atom has the same number of protons and electrons.

Use the sliders to construct the neutral atom described by the parameters below.

Your target atom is {@atom_name@}-{@mass_number@}.

Adjust the model until it matches these parameters:

  • The element name is {@atom_name@}.
  • The mass number is {@mass_number@}.
  • The charge is 0.

When the model is correct, enter the final answers below and submit. STACK will check your final input.

[[jsxgraph input-ref-ans1="pInput" input-ref-ans2="nInput" input-ref-ans3="eInput" width="100%" height="620px"]] var root = document.getElementById(divid); root.style.border = "1px solid #dbe3ee"; root.style.borderRadius = "16px"; root.style.background = "linear-gradient(#ffffff,#fbfcfe)"; root.style.overflow = "hidden"; var pStack = document.getElementById(pInput); var nStack = document.getElementById(nInput); var eStack = document.getElementById(eInput); var elementNames = { 1: ["H", "Hydrogen"], 2: ["He", "Helium"], 3: ["Li", "Lithium"], 4: ["Be", "Beryllium"], 5: ["B", "Boron"], 6: ["C", "Carbon"], 7: ["N", "Nitrogen"], 8: ["O", "Oxygen"], 9: ["F", "Fluorine"], 10: ["Ne", "Neon"], 11: ["Na", "Sodium"], 12: ["Mg", "Magnesium"], 13: ["Al", "Aluminium"], 14: ["Si", "Silicon"], 15: ["P", "Phosphorus"], 16: ["S", "Sulfur"], 17: ["Cl", "Chlorine"], 18: ["Ar", "Argon"] }; var state = { p: {#start_protons#}, n: {#start_neutrons#}, e: {#start_electrons#} }; var animationPhase = 0; var radii = [2, 3.3, 4.6]; var board = JXG.JSXGraph.initBoard(divid, { boundingbox: [-7.2, 6.8, 7.2, -6.8], axis: false, showCopyright: false, showNavigation: false, keepAspectRatio: true }); var pSlider = board.create("slider", [[-5.8, -5.2], [-1.6, -5.2], [1, state.p, 18]], { name: "Protons", snapWidth: 1 }); var nSlider = board.create("slider", [[-5.8, -5.9], [-1.6, -5.9], [0, state.n, 22]], { name: "Neutrons", snapWidth: 1 }); var eSlider = board.create("slider", [[-5.8, -6.6], [-1.6, -6.6], [1, state.e, 18]], { name: "Electrons", snapWidth: 1 }); stack_jxg.bind_slider(pInput, pSlider); stack_jxg.bind_slider(nInput, nSlider); stack_jxg.bind_slider(eInput, eSlider); stack_jxg.define_group([pSlider, nSlider, eSlider]); stack_jxg.starts_moved(pSlider); stack_jxg.starts_moved(nSlider); stack_jxg.starts_moved(eSlider); /* Main atom diagram */ board.create("circle", [[0,0],1], { strokeColor:"#1976d2", strokeWidth:2, fillOpacity:0.06, fixed:true }); var shell1 = board.create("circle", [[0,0],2], {dash:2, strokeColor:"#1e88e5", fixed:true}); var shell2 = board.create("circle", [[0,0],3.3], {dash:2, strokeColor:"#1e88e5", fixed:true}); var shell3 = board.create("circle", [[0,0],4.6], {dash:2, strokeColor:"#1e88e5", fixed:true}); board.create("text", [-0.28,0.14, function(){ return elementNames[state.p][0]; }], {fontSize:24, fixed:true}); /* Slider labels, aligned with the controls in the lower-left corner. */ board.create("text", [-6.95,-5.05, function(){ return "Protons: " + Math.round(pSlider.Value()); }], {fontSize:14, fixed:true}); board.create("text", [-6.95,-5.75, function(){ return "Neutrons: " + Math.round(nSlider.Value()); }], {fontSize:14, fixed:true}); board.create("text", [-6.95,-6.45, function(){ return "Electrons: " + Math.round(eSlider.Value()); }], {fontSize:14, fixed:true}); board.create("text", [3.8,6.25, function(){ return "Element: " + elementNames[Math.round(pSlider.Value())][0]; }], {fontSize:14, fixed:true}); board.create("text", [3.8,5.75, function(){ return "Name: " + elementNames[Math.round(pSlider.Value())][1]; }], {fontSize:14, fixed:true}); board.create("text", [3.8,5.25, function(){ return "Mass: " + (Math.round(pSlider.Value()) + Math.round(nSlider.Value())); }], {fontSize:14, fixed:true}); board.create("text", [3.8,4.75, function(){ var ch = Math.round(pSlider.Value()) - Math.round(eSlider.Value()); return "Charge: " + (ch > 0 ? "+" + ch : ch); }], {fontSize:14, fixed:true}); function nucleusCoordinate(index, total, axis, kind) { var count = Math.max(total, 1); var theta = (index * 2.399963229728653 + (kind === "p" ? 0.35 : 1.05)) % (2 * Math.PI); var ring = Math.floor(index / 6); var radius = 0.08 + ring * 0.12 + (index % 3) * 0.018; var r = Math.min(radius, 0.42) * (kind === "p" ? 1 : 0.97); if (axis === 0) { return r * Math.cos(theta); } return r * Math.sin(theta); } function shellData(index, electronCount) { if (index < 2) { return {shell:0, pos:index, count:Math.min(electronCount,2), speed:0.9}; } if (index < 10) { return {shell:1, pos:index-2, count:Math.max(Math.min(electronCount-2,8),1), speed:0.6}; } return {shell:2, pos:index-10, count:Math.max(electronCount-10,1), speed:0.4}; } function electronCoordinate(index, axis) { var electronCount = Math.round(eSlider.Value()); if (index >= electronCount) { return 20; } var data = shellData(index, electronCount); var baseAngle = (2*Math.PI*data.pos)/data.count; var angle = baseAngle + animationPhase*data.speed; if (axis === 0) { return radii[data.shell]*Math.cos(angle); } return radii[data.shell]*Math.sin(angle); } function updateShellVisibility() { var electronCount = Math.round(eSlider.Value()); shell1.setAttribute({visible: electronCount > 0}); shell2.setAttribute({visible: electronCount > 2}); shell3.setAttribute({visible: electronCount > 10}); } function updateNucleusVisibility() { var protonCount = Math.round(pSlider.Value()); var neutronCount = Math.round(nSlider.Value()); for (var i = 0; i < 18; i++) { if (nucleusProtons[i]) { nucleusProtons[i].setAttribute({ visible: i < protonCount }); } } for (var j = 0; j < 22; j++) { if (nucleusNeutrons[j]) { nucleusNeutrons[j].setAttribute({ visible: j < neutronCount }); } } } var nucleusProtons = []; for (var p = 0; p < 18; p++) { (function(k) { nucleusProtons[k] = board.create("point", [ function(){ return nucleusCoordinate(k, Math.round(pSlider.Value()), 0, "p"); }, function(){ return nucleusCoordinate(k, Math.round(pSlider.Value()), 1, "p"); } ], { size: 4.4, name: "", withLabel: false, fixed: true, fillColor: "#ef5350", strokeColor: "#c62828" }); })(p); } var nucleusNeutrons = []; for (var n = 0; n < 22; n++) { (function(k) { nucleusNeutrons[k] = board.create("point", [ function(){ return nucleusCoordinate(k, Math.round(nSlider.Value()), 0, "n"); }, function(){ return nucleusCoordinate(k, Math.round(nSlider.Value()), 1, "n"); } ], { size: 4.4, name: "", withLabel: false, fixed: true, fillColor: "#42a5f5", strokeColor: "#1565c0" }); })(n); } for (var i=0; i<18; i++) { (function(k) { board.create("point", [ function(){ return electronCoordinate(k,0); }, function(){ return electronCoordinate(k,1); } ], { size:3, name:"", withLabel:false, fixed:true, fillColor:"#111111", strokeColor:"#111111" }); })(i); } updateShellVisibility(); updateNucleusVisibility(); board.update(); function animate() { animationPhase = animationPhase + 0.03; updateShellVisibility(); updateNucleusVisibility(); board.update(); window.requestAnimationFrame(animate); } window.requestAnimationFrame(animate); [[/jsxgraph]]

[[input:ans1]] [[validation:ans1]] [[input:ans2]] [[validation:ans2]] [[input:ans3]] [[validation:ans3]]

[[feedback:prt1]]

generalfeedback.text:
For a neutral atom of {@atom_name@}-{@mass_number@}:

  • Protons = atomic number = {@ta1@}
  • Neutrons = mass number − atomic number = {@mass_number@} − {@atomic_number@} = {@ta2@}
  • Electrons = protons for a neutral atom = {@ta3@}

questiondescription.text: Interactive JSXGraph activity where students use sliders to build a neutral isotope and observe element, mass and charge.

prtcorrect.text: Correct answer, well done.

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Your answer is partially correct.

prtincorrect.text: Incorrect answer.

node.description: Checks the proton, neutron and electron sliders.

falsefeedback.text:
Check the sliders again.

  • Protons determine the element.
  • Mass number = protons + neutrons.
  • For a neutral atom, electrons = protons.

For this atom, the target is: protons = {@ta1@}, neutrons = {@ta2@}, electrons = {@ta3@}.
